t_ch: 那就别磨蹭了，快点出发吧。
t_en: Alright, then let's cut the chat and move out!

t_ch: 有点好奇拳手们的实力。
t_en: I'm curious to see how strong the boxers are.

t_ch: 哼，好奇就自己来买票看，看不出来就别怪自己没眼力劲儿。
t_en: Hmph, if you're curious, then buy a ticket and see for yourself. If you still can't tell how strong they are, don't blame me.

t_ch: 不服？不服气就自己上擂台试试，打几场就知道了。
t_en: Not convinced? Then enter the ring and fight a few bouts yourself, and then you'll see.

t_ch: 欺负弱者不算真正的拳击。
t_en: I'm way above beating up weaklings.

t_ch: 哈，说得冠冕堂皇的，你该不会是怂了吧？
t_en: Ha! You talk cocky, but is that just because you're scared you'll lose?

t_ch: 连上去打拳都不敢，还说什么「真正的拳击」，装得一副很懂的样子，吓唬谁呢？
t_en: You don't dare enter the ring, and then you talk all high-and-mighty about my boxers being "weaklings." Am I supposed to feel intimidated?

t_ch: 想骗我帮你打白工？
t_en: Are you trying to trick me into free labor?

t_ch: 哼！现在的年轻人，一点亏都吃不得…以后的路还长，你这样斤斤计较，以后可是走不远的。
t_en: Hmph! Young people these days. If it's not to their benefit, count them out... Life is a long journey. You won't make it far if you're this fussy about everything.

t_ch: 找什么借口，说到底不就是没那个胆吗？
t_en: Excuse after excuse. Really, you just don't have the courage, huh?

t_ch: 我只是不想和普通人打。
t_en: I just don't want to fight against ordinary people.

t_ch: 我这儿的拳手可不是什么普通人，没点名气都踏不进俱乐部的大门。
t_en: My boxers here aren't just "ordinary people." Only reputed fighters are allowed to step into this club.

t_ch: 我怕弄脏我的手。
t_en: I don't want to get my hands dirty.

t_ch: 哈！口气还挺大。
t_en: Ha! You've got some nerve.

t_ch: 你去打听打听，看看我这「搏击俱乐部」为什么在这地下远近闻名——观众买票进来可不是为了看什么无名小卒！
t_en: Ask anyone and you'll learn why my Fight Club is known far and wide across the underground. People don't buy tickets to come watch some unknown amateurs!

t_ch: 你既然这么自信，那正好，现在俱乐部里恰巧有几个拳手在训练。
t_en: You're so confident, huh? It just so happens that we have a few boxers in the club right now who are all eager for some hands-on practice.

t_ch: 只要你能打败他们，我就给你一笔奖金，算是添个彩头。
t_en: I'll give you a nice little reward if you can beat them, just in case you needed any extra incentive.

t_ch: 怎么样，敢不敢来一场？
t_en: What do you think? Bold enough to fight a round?

t_ch: 我考虑一下吧。
t_en: Let me think about it.

t_ch: 还要怎么考虑？照你的话说，这对你不是小菜一碟吗？
t_en: Think about it? According to you, wouldn't they be a piece of cake for you?

t_ch: 我赶时间，速战速决。
t_en: I'm in a hurry. I'll make quick work of them.

t_ch: 哼，像你这种过度自信的年轻人，我可见得太多了。
t_en: Hmph. I've seen a lot of overconfident young people like yourself.

t_ch: 嘿！你准备好打一场漂亮的比赛了吗？
t_en: Hey! Ready for a good one now?

t_ch: 我准备好了。
t_en: Ready.

t_ch: 稍微再等一下。
t_en: Wait a minute.

t_ch: 备受瞩目的新秀出现了——
t_en: Coming up— a much anticipated high profile rookie!

t_ch: 是…天赋异禀，领跑俱乐部排名的全新赛季？
t_en: Is {M#he}{F#she}... a gifted new talent ready to rock the club's new seasonal rankings?

t_ch: 还是…自不量力，除了徒增新伤之外白跑一趟？
t_en: Or is {M#he}{F#she}... going beyond {M#his}{F#her} depth just to get some new scars for a show?

t_ch: 让我们拭目以待，看看这位超级新秀今天能交出怎样的答卷！有请——{NICKNAME}！
t_en: Let's wait and see what the new rookie has in store for us today! Please welcome— {NICKNAME}!

t_ch: 这流畅的战斗…令人窒息的压迫感…甚至让我感觉历代拳王也不过如此！了不起，实在是了不起！
t_en: The smoothness of the battle... the suffocating sense of oppression... even the old champs can't do much better! Impressive, most impressive!

t_ch: 欢呼声在哪里？你们都没吃饭吗？再响点，再来一轮！掌声和欢呼声送给史考特俱乐部冉冉升起的超级新星——{NICKNAME}！
t_en: Where are your cheers? Did you not eat? Louder, encore! Applause and cheers to Scott Club's newest rising superstar— {NICKNAME}!

t_ch: （…该去找史考特了，不知道他准备了多少奖金……）
t_en: (... Time to find Scott, I wonder how much is in the prize...)

t_ch: 我赢了，说好的奖金…
t_en: I won, so the money...

t_ch: 哈哈哈，我没看错，不愧是「外来者」！我看你年纪不大，身手倒是不同凡响，比那几个只会按固定套路出拳的土鳖强多了。
t_en: Hahaha, I was right about you, you're quite a fighter! Green but extraordinary, much better than those boring old farts with fixed routines.

t_ch: 至于奖励…放心好了，肯定会给你的，不过……
t_en: As for the prize money... don't worry, you'll definitely get some, but...

t_ch: 不过什么？
t_en: But what?

t_ch: 没什么，只是流程上会可能有一点麻烦，不是什么大事。
t_en: It's nothing. It's just that there may be a little trouble in the process of securing the funds. No biggie.

t_ch: 别废话了，我奖金呢？
t_en: Cut to the chase. Where's my money?

t_ch: 急什么？没看到我这么大个人站在你面前吗？我又不会跑，催什么催。
t_en: What's the hurry? Can't you see I'm right here? I ain't going nowhere, don't rush me.

t_ch: 最近…呃，拳馆的利润水平不是特别理想。我现在只能给你发一部分，至于尾款……
t_en: It's just that lately... the boxing gym hasn't exactly been printing money. I can give you some now, but the rest...

t_ch: 你先帮我个忙，我现在得找一位特别的「拳手」，之后我再把尾款补上。放心，这事对你来说轻松得很，我保证！
t_en: How about you do me a favor first? I'm now in need of a special boxer. If you can find one, I'll give you the rest of the money. Easy eh? I promise!

t_ch: 你有那么多拳手还不够？
t_en: Don't you have enough boxers already?

t_ch: 你见过那群土鳖吗？整天就知道埋头训练，个个搞得灰头土脸…他们根本不懂观众想看的是什么。
t_en: Have you seen those knuckleheads? All they know is to bury their heads and train, getting all dirty and sweaty... They don't know what the audience wants to see.

t_ch: 观众想要的是刺激，懂吗？刺激！感官上的冲击，视觉上的刺激，我得找一帮懂观众喜好的拳手才行！
t_en: Audiences want excitement. Excitement! Sensory feast, visual stimuli. I need someone who can stand in the ring and knows what the audience wants!

t_ch: 为什么非得是我？
t_en: Why me?

t_ch: 你以为我是在故意针对你？是，我是签了不少拳手，但那帮家伙除了按训练里学到的套路出拳以外，啥也不会！稍有点品位的观众早就看腻那群小丑了。
t_en: You think it's about you? Nah. Yes, I may have signed a few boxers, but they know nothing but cliched routines from their training! Any audience with a bit of taste would've already been sick of these clowns.

t_ch: 这种拳手，要来何用？所以我上个月就给他们发了降薪通知书…要么自愿降薪，要么乖乖滚蛋！
t_en: So what's the point of keeping them around? That's why I sent a message to everyone last month... They can either take a pay cut voluntarily or kiss their sweet butts goodbye.

t_ch: 冷静一点…
t_en: Calm down...

t_ch: 你是不知道养这群无耻的寄生虫有多难，吃我的用我的，哼……
t_en: You don't know what's it like to raise these shameless parasites, everything they eat and use is mine... hmph...

t_ch: 拳手们也有自己的追求。
t_en: They also have their dreams.

t_ch: 别跟我提什么个人追求，观众们会在乎你的「个人追求」吗？大家之所以会来这儿，就是想得到最直观的感官冲击，懂吗？
t_en: Don't talk about dreams with me. You think audiences care about your dreams? They're just here for a visceral sensory experience, capeesh?

t_ch: 哼…如果不能引领潮流，就注定会被时代淘汰…是时候了！我要探索全新的商业模式……
t_en: Hmph... In this world, you either lead or die... so it's time for me to start exploring a completely new business model...

t_ch: 对了，我知道你去过裂界，那你肯定见过那种怪物吧？有着人的身形，穿着特别的盔甲……
t_en: By the way, I know you've been to the Fragmentum, so you must've seen those monsters, right? Shaped like a human, wearing special armor...

t_ch: 这事对你来说肯定轻而易举——只要你能带回一套怪物的盔甲，我就把奖金的尾款发给你。
t_en: I'm sure it's easy for you — just get me a set of that armor and I'll give you the rest of your prize money.

t_ch: 还愣着干嘛？快去快回，你晚回来一天，拳馆就要养这群寄生虫一天，再这么下去我可就亏大了！
t_en: So what are you waiting for? Get moving, the longer you take, the longer I need to feed those parasites. What a filthy waste of money!

t_ch: 你要的盔甲。
t_en: Here's your armor.

t_ch: 就是它，就是它！这质感没得说！
t_en: That's it, that's it! Wow, that texture!

t_ch: 你这家伙，虽然态度让人不爽，但是办事效率确实没的说…我没看错人，嚯哈哈！
t_en: You had an annoying attitude but you still got the job done... I was right about you, ah—haha!

t_ch: 拿着，之前答应给你的奖金尾款，我史考特说到做到。以后有机会了，可以来找我谈谈生意，咱们互惠互利——嚯哈哈哈！
t_en: Here's the rest of your money like promised. Scott is always true to his word. If we can get a chance in the future, then come and talk business with me. We can help each other out— muhahaha!

t_ch: 这怪物的盔甲到底拿来做什么？
t_en: What do you plan to do with the monster armor?

t_ch: 拿来穿呗，不然呢？难不成就放在门口做摆设吗？
t_en: To wear it of course, or what? Leave it around as house decor?

t_ch: 难道你有收集盔甲的爱好？
t_en: Do you have a hobby of collecting armor?

t_ch: 嗐，我只有赚钱这一个爱好。
t_en: Hey, I only have a hobby of making money.

t_ch: 哎，你既然帮了我的忙，我就直接告诉你吧——我打算找个拳手，让他打扮成怪物的样子上擂台打拳。
t_en: Hey, since you helped me, I'll let you in on my plan. I'm going to have a boxer dress up as a monster to fight in the ring.

t_ch: 名字我都想好了，就叫「怪物盔甲」，到时候门票肯定大卖，拳赛场场都爆满！
t_en: I've already thought up a name: "Monster Armor." Tickets will definitely be sold out by then, and the boxing arena will be full!

t_ch: 嚯哈哈哈，怎么样？这么好的点子，全下层只有我史考特想得出来！
t_en: Hahaha, what do you think? In all of the Underworld, only Scott can come up with such an ingenious idea!

t_ch: 这不太合适吧？
t_en: Isn't it unsuitable for people to wear, though?

t_ch: 怪物身上的东西不嫌脏吗？
t_en: Isn't it disgusting to wear stuff ripped off monsters?

t_ch: 行了行了，你货都交了，就别啰嗦了，奖金拿好！这么磨叽无非就是想找我多要点好处，你那点小心思我还不知道？
t_en: Hey hey, you've done your part of the job, so shut up and take your money! All this jibber-jabber is just you trying to squeeze me for more. Don't think I don't know what's on your mind!

t_ch: 这套盔甲就是拿来穿的，只要有人穿了它，我就能赚钱。
t_en: Armor is supposed to be worn. As long as someone's wearing it, then I can make money.

t_ch: 用来拍特摄的话差条腰带。
t_en: It'll need a belt to serve as a Tokusatsu costume.

t_ch: 特摄？那是什么东西？
t_en: Tokusatsu? What's that?

t_ch: 这套盔甲就是拿来给人穿的。
t_en: This armor is to be worn.

t_ch: 有什么不合适的？哦，你说这盔甲的尺寸？看上去的确大了点，拿去给铁匠随便改改就行了。
t_en: What's unsuitable? Oh, you mean the size of this armor? It does look a little big, so just take it to the blacksmith and get it resized.

t_ch: 又不是我自己穿，要那么干净做什么呢？
t_en: It's not like I'm gonna wear it myself, so why does it need to be clean?

t_ch: 感觉这不是人应该穿的。
t_en: I still don't think people should wear it.

t_ch: 你说的有道理，但是关我屁事。
t_en: You're right, but it's none of my business.

t_ch: 我史考特也不是黑心老板，看在你确实帮了我个忙，而且也有点实力的份上，我额外送你个「特殊礼遇」。
t_en: Scott is no cruel boss, you know. Since you've really helped me, and proven yourself capable, I'll give you a little extra "house special."

t_ch: 以后你来拳馆，要是你看到「掘掘博士」也在里面，可以找他帮你清点下人员，安排个拳馆挑战赛什么的，就不用再排队买票了。
t_en: Next time you come around, if you see Dr. Dig here, get him to do a head count and squeeze you into a challenge round or something like that. That way, you won't need to line up for tickets.

t_ch: 这下你该满意了吧？别再来烦我了，拿着奖金回去歇着吧！嗯…接下来…该让哪个废物试试这套盔甲呢……
t_en: Are we good now? Now get out of my face with your money! Hmmmm... so now... which poor fool should try this armor on...

t_ch: 拳手们还在休息，看比赛或者想打挑战赛的请稍后再来排队。
t_en: The boxers are still resting. If you are watching the match or want to play in a challenge match, please come back later to wait in line.

t_ch: 史考特说我不用排队…
t_en: Scott said I don't have to queue...

t_ch: 我有那个…「特殊礼遇」。
t_en: I have that... "house special."

t_ch: 哟，原来是大名鼎鼎的{NICKNAME}啊，刚才忙晕了没仔细看，失敬失敬！
t_en: Oh! It's the famous {NICKNAME}! Sorry I didn't recognize you right away.

t_ch: 老板和我说过了，「特殊礼遇」其实就是贵宾级待遇嘛，您来了就不用排队，哪个拳手有空就给您安排哪位。
t_en: Boss already told me that the house special is for VIPs, so of course I won't keep you waiting. I'll get you the next available boxer.

t_ch: 您有空的话多来啊，不用客气。老板说了，您来打挑战赛就等于免费给拳手们指导业务，我们巴不得您多来喂喂招儿。
t_en: And please make yourself at home. Boss said you coming to challenges is like free training for these boxers. We are very grateful for your visits.

t_ch: {NICKNAME}，又是一场毫无悬念的胜利！王者般的实力恐怖如斯！
t_en: {NICKNAME}, another victory! What a majestic performance!

t_ch: 你还有想亲自指导的拳手吗？没有的话也可以在拳馆里到处转转，别见外！老板嘱咐过了，无任欢迎！
t_en: Is there any other boxer you'd like to challenge? If not, feel free to look around! Boss said you are always welcomed here.

t_ch: 让我再来一场吧
t_en: Get me another one.

t_ch: 没有了，我到旁边逛逛。
t_en: No, I'll have a look around.

t_ch: 出事了可别来找我。
t_en: Don't come to me if something goes wrong.

t_ch: 我对我手下那帮人的状况门儿清，能有什么事。
t_en: I know my men. What could go wrong?

t_ch: 哎，说了不听那就算了…
t_en: Hey, if you don't wanna listen, that's fine...

t_ch: 我手下的人什么样儿我还不清楚？你说的这些纯属瞎操心，别费这个劲了。
t_en: Hey, I know my boys, no need for you to get all worked up for nothing.

t_ch: 别在我耳边叽叽歪歪了，听着烦死了。
t_en: All that whining is hard on the ears.

t_ch: （那边穿着盔甲的…史考特这么快就安排好了拳手？）
t_en: (The one in armor over there... Scott found a boxer for that already?)

t_ch: 你哪位？找我有什么事吗？
t_en: Who are you? What do you want?

t_ch: 你就是史考特选的人？
t_en: So you're the one Scott picked?

t_ch: 算是吧，史考特老板找了我们这帮拳手来试盔甲，我刚好最合适。
t_en: More or less. Scott got a whole bunch of boxers to try out the armor and I was the best fit.

t_ch: 史考特跟你说过盔甲的事吗？
t_en: Did Scott tell you about the armor?

t_ch: 有说过一些，比如要我穿上盔甲装成裂界里的怪物什么的。
t_en: A little, like asking me to put on the armor and become a monster from the Fragmentum or something.

t_ch: 还有别的事吗？如果没什么急事，请不要打扰我，我还在适应这套盔甲……
t_en: Anything else? Don't bother me if it's nothing urgent, I'm still getting used to this armor...

t_ch: 盔甲是裂界产物，谨慎点用。
t_en: It's from the Fragmentum. Be careful.

t_ch: …裂界里的东西，就没有一样是不危险的，我早就习惯了。
t_en: ...Everything in the Fragmentum is dangerous. I'm used to it.

t_ch: 只要合身，能让我工作下去就行，目前好像也没什么大问题。
t_en: As long as it fits and pays, no biggie right now.

t_ch: 你穿了这套盔甲多久？
t_en: How long have you been wearing this armor?

t_ch: 没多久，也就打了一两场吧。
t_en: Not long, a game or two.

t_ch: 真有观众喜欢看？
t_en: Audience actually buy it?

t_ch: 还挺多的，现在反响还挺好，观众们都是图个新鲜，很正常。
t_en: Quite a lot actually, at least for now. Audiences are just looking for something new, as is typical.

t_ch: 穿上后有不良反应吗？
t_en: Any adverse reactions after wearing it?

t_ch: 不良反应？好像没有，就是穿起来不太透气，在擂台上行走不太方便。
t_en: Adverse reactions? Don't think so. It's just not very breathable and not easy to walk around with in the ring.

t_ch: 不打扰你了。
t_en: I'll leave you to it.

t_ch: 手臂这里的甲板好像有点松了，算了，我还是自己调整一下吧……
t_en: The arm plates seem to be a little loose. Forget it, I'll just fix it myself...

t_ch: 你也是来看「怪物盔甲」的吗？哎，有他在的场次，票可真难抢。
t_en: Did you come to see Monster Armor too? It's so hard to get tickets for his matches.

t_ch: 你是来看「怪物盔甲」的吗？
t_en: You're here for Monster Armor?

t_ch: 呃…说不上专门来看他，但听说他挺有看头，呃，怎么说呢。
t_en: Errrr, not specifically, but I heard he's quite something... Errr... I don't know.

t_ch: 大家来拳馆不都是图一乐吗，不会有人真的喜欢上「怪物盔甲」了吧？
t_en: People are just here for some fun. Nobody will actually like Monster Armor, right?

t_ch: 说起来…「怪物盔甲」后天还有几场比赛来着？怎么这票价越来越高了……
t_en: By the way... How many matches are there for Monster Armor after this? Why is the price getting higher and higher...

t_ch: 你看了上一场比赛吗？如何评价前任拳王全程黑脸，被「怪物盔甲」暴揍？
t_en: Did you watch the last match? What did you think about Monster Armor knocking the brains out of the ex-champ?

t_ch: 「怪物盔甲」才赢了几场而已，看你激动成这样，差不多得了！
t_en: Monster Armor's only got a few wins. Look at you all worked up!

t_ch: 你们是来看「怪物盔甲」的吗？
t_en: Are you here to see Monster Armor?

t_ch: 这不废话嘛，我可是「怪物盔甲」的粉丝！我觉得他出拳的风格大开大合，很有气势，最近战绩也很好，未来可期！
t_en: Heck yeah, I'm a Monster Armor fan! I think his style is very wild and imposing, and he's been on a roll lately. That boy's got a future!

t_ch: 算了吧，虽然「怪物盔甲」上一场打得很不错，但也别瞎吹什么未来可期。
t_en: Come on, just because he did alright in the last game, that doesn't make him a future rock star.

t_ch: 你什么意思？「怪物盔甲」什么实力大家都有目共睹，比以前那几个又菜又装的家伙可强太多了。
t_en: What do you mean? Monster Armor obviously has what it takes. He's far better than those pretentious and useless old farts from before.

t_ch: 下一把的对手可是五冠王，懂不懂冠军的含金量？
t_en: The next opponent is a five-time champion. Do you know what it means to be a champ?

t_ch: 什么狗屁五冠王，「怪物盔甲」来了，其他人都得跪着求饶。
t_en: Yeah five-time champ my butt. He'll be kneeling to Monster Armor and begging for mercy.

t_ch: 别急，等拳王把伤养好了，就让你知道什么叫残忍。
t_en: Just you wait till the champ has recovered, you'll know what real brutality looks like.

t_ch: 我这就去把后面几场的票定了，等着看我「怪物盔甲」大佬夺冠吧！
t_en: I'm going to get the tickets for the next few games, and wait to see my Monster Armor win the championship!

t_ch: 别吵别吵，我正在回忆去年卫冕冠军赛的细节，这下都被你给吵没了！
t_en: Shush, I'm trying to remember the details of the last championship, but you're disrupting my train of thought!

t_ch: 你谁啊？我已经买了季票了，不想听票贩子推销。
t_en: I've already bought a season pass so that I don't have to deal with scalpers.

t_ch: 你是「怪物盔甲」的粉丝吗？
t_en: Are you a fan of Monster Armor?

t_ch: 粉丝？什么粉丝？拳赛只是消遣而已，我劝各位还是不要带入太多感情……
t_en: Fan? What fan? It's just games, don't drag yourself too deep into it...

t_ch: 不过我得承认，看这「怪物」打拳属实有点意思，也难怪有这么多人追捧那家伙。
t_en: But I have to admit, it's quite interesting to watch this monster punch, and it's no wonder this many people are cheering for him.

t_ch: 还好我早早地买了季票，可以直接去二楼贵宾席，不用跟这群家伙抢票……
t_en: Good thing I bought the season pass early, and I can go directly to the VIP seat on the second floor without having to fight for tickets with these guys...

t_ch: 「怪物盔甲」现在怎么样了？
t_en: How is Monster Armor now?

t_ch: 还行吧，火过一段时间以后，现在已经有点过气了…但这可难不倒我。
t_en: He's alright, he's off the peak now but that's nothing to worry about.

t_ch: 接下来，我准备安排他去跟机器人打两场，把拳赛的话题度再炒起来。
t_en: I'll get him to fight a robot a few times next, just to heat things up again.

t_ch: 我担心盔甲的污染会影响他…
t_en: I'm worried about contamination by the armor...

t_ch: 污染？别逗了，盔甲能有什么污染，我看那家伙健壮得离谱，徒手就能把机器人给拆干净。
t_en: Contamination? Don't be ridiculous, what kind of contamination can the armor have? I think that guy is ridiculously strong. He can smash the robot with his bare hands.

t_ch: 我去看看他的身体情况…
t_en: I'll go and check on him.

t_ch: 我签拳手是为了挣钱，不是给自己请了个祖宗！他的身体哪有那么娇贵！
t_en: I sign boxers to make money, not to treat them like babies! He's not that delicate!

t_ch: 与其可怜那家伙，不如来可怜可怜花了一大笔冤枉钱的我！
t_en: Instead of pitying that guy, you should feel sorry for me, who wasted all that money!

t_ch: 真的吗？我不信。
t_en: Oh yeah? I don't believe you.

t_ch: 不信拉倒！相信我，这些个拳手老粗根本不懂什么叫感恩，不值得你浪费时间。
t_en: Believe it or not, these boxer scums know nothing about being grateful. He's not worth your time.

t_ch: 哎——他今天正好有比赛，人就在里面。有什么你自己跟他说去吧，别烦我了。
t_en: You know what, he's actually got a match today, and he's in there. Go and talk to him, just leave me alone.

t_ch: …有什么事吗？
t_en: ...Can I help you?

t_ch: 身体还好吗？
t_en: How are you feeling?

t_ch: …就那样呗，还行。
t_en: Same old, nothing much.

t_ch: 谢谢你的好意，不过我…咳…我身体好得很…咳…没什么大问题。
t_en: Thank you for your kindness, but I... *cough*... I'm fine... *cough*... It's no biggie.

t_ch: 我是特意来看望你的。
t_en: I came here to see you.

t_ch: …我很好，没必要特意探望。
t_en: ...I'm fine, no need to worry.

t_ch: 谢谢你的好意，不过我…咳…我身体好得很…咳…没什么大问题。
t_en: Thank you for your kindness, but I... *cough*... I'm fine... *cough*... It's no biggie.

t_ch: 我只是来看看盔甲的。
t_en: I just came to see the armor.

t_ch: …这怪物的盔甲吓到了不少人，也赚了不少钱。
t_en: This monster armor scared a lot of people and made a lot of money.

t_ch: …咳…要是我会错意了，你就当我没说…我身体好得很…咳…没什么大问题。
t_en: *cough*... Forget I said anything if I misunderstood you... I'm alright... *cough*... Nothing wrong.

t_ch: 你身子很虚，去看看医生吧。
t_en: You are very weak. You should see a doctor.

t_ch: 不，我好得很，不劳你费神了。
t_en: No, I'm fine, thanks.

t_ch: 盔甲对人可能有不良影响…
t_en: The armor may have adverse effects on people...

t_ch: 哪有什么不良影响，我说过了，我身体好得很，状态也很不错…咳…还能继续打拳。
t_en: There are no adverse effects. As I said, I'm in good health, and good shape... *cough* I can continue to fight.

t_ch: 咳…接下来还有几场和机器人的比赛，我得去准备……
t_en: Ahem... I have a few matches with robots in a bit, I need to prepare...

t_ch: 咳！咳咳咳——
t_en: Cough! Cough cough—

t_ch: 喂，「怪物盔甲」！醒醒，给我醒醒！
t_en: Hey, Monster Armor! Wake up, wake up!

t_ch: 他怎么倒下了，是体力不行了么？
t_en: Why did he fall? Is something wrong?

t_ch: （必须带他去诊所检查……）
t_en: (I have to take him to the clinic for a checkup...)

t_ch: 你带着科鲁泽来到娜塔莎的诊所里，他那副虚弱的样子实在让你不放心。
t_en: You bring Kluzer to Natasha's clinic. His frail appearance really bothers you.

t_ch: 哦？{NICKNAME}，你的这位朋友是发生了什么情况？
t_en: Oh? {NICKNAME}, what happened to this friend of yours?

t_ch: （向娜塔莎讲述事情经过）
t_en: (You tell Natasha what happened)

t_ch: 从裂界怪物身上获得的盔甲？这么危险的东西也敢穿在身上……
t_en: Armor from Fragmentum monsters? He's really got some guts to walk around wearing such a dangerous thing...

t_ch: 史考特老板跟我说，穿这套盔甲打拳赛，底薪就给我多加一点…咳咳……
t_en: Mr. Scott told me that if I wear this armor to matches, I will get a raise... *cough*

t_ch: 为了这么点钱，连命都要赔进去？
t_en: Is the money worth your life?

t_ch: …我有很多地方…咳咳…要用钱，没办法。
t_en: ...There's a lot of holes I need to fill... *cough* with money...

t_ch: 咳咳，看完了吗，医生？能让我回去了吗？
t_en: *cough* *cough* Are we done now, doc? Can I go back now?

t_ch: 等等，要先看看诊断结果。
t_en: Wait, let's read the diagnosis first.

t_ch: 嗯，报告是出来了，但这情况…实在是有点糟糕。
t_en: Well, the report is out, but the situation... is really not good.

t_ch: 你还不能回去。
t_en: You can't go back yet.

t_ch: 医生还没说话呢…不过，你这一身情况确实不能回去。
t_en: The doc hasn't spoken yet... and you really can't go back like this.

t_ch: 你看这里，这几处都是旧伤，按理说早该痊愈了，但伤口不仅没愈合，表面还有像结晶一样的东西析出。
t_en: Look here, these are all old wounds which should have healed long ago, but not only did the wounds not heal, there are crystal-like things on the surface.

t_ch: 我检测过了，这些结晶物和裂界里的碎屑物质成分相似度很高。我见过一些受裂界侵蚀影响的患者，你现在的症状和他们很像。
t_en: I have checked, and these crystals are very similar in composition to the detritus in the Fragmentum. I've seen some patients affected by Fragmentum corrosion and your current symptoms are similar to theirs.

t_ch: 侵蚀就像某种慢性的毒素，它们会不断干扰伤口的正常愈合，继而增加器官的负担，严重的话可能会导致器官衰竭。
t_en: Corrosion is like a kind of chronic toxin. It constantly interferes with the normal healing process of wounds, which in turn increases the burden on the organs, and can lead to organ failure in severe cases.

t_ch: 总之，你不能再碰这套盔甲了，搏击活动也必须先停下。好好休息，把伤养好了再来我这儿复查一次。
t_en: In short, you can no longer touch this armor, and you must cease all fighting activities for the time being. Get some good rest, recover from your injuries, and come back to me for follow up.

t_ch: …真的不能再打拳了吗？
t_en: ...So I can't fight anymore?

t_ch: 不是我危言耸听，再这么下去，你迟早会倒在擂台上——永远都起不来的那种。明白了吗？
t_en: I'm not being alarmist. If you go on like this, sooner or later you'll fall in the ring — the kind of fall you'll never recover from. Do you understand?

t_ch: …知道了，医生。
t_en: ...Yes doc.

t_ch: 那我先走了…谢谢你，陌生人。
t_en: I'll leave now then... Thank you, stranger.

t_ch: 科鲁泽拖着受伤的身体离开了诊所……
t_en: Kluzer left the clinic dragging his injured body...

t_ch: 哎，这样的病人我见过太多了，多半不会听从医嘱……
t_en: You know, I've seen too many patients like this, and most of them won't follow the doctor's orders...

t_ch: {NICKNAME}，先别走。我这儿正好还有些事，想找你帮个忙。
t_en: {NICKNAME}, don't go yet. I need to ask you a favor.

t_ch: 什么事？
t_en: What is it?

t_ch: 哪有这么巧的事儿？
t_en: Wow, how convenient... for you.

t_ch: 就是有这么巧，你先听我说完。
t_en: Well, it's my lucky day. Now listen to me.

t_ch: 大概是从前不久开始，每隔几天就会有装满物资的包裹被送到诊所来。
t_en: A while ago, someone began sending a package full of supplies to the clinic every few days.

t_ch: 里面装的东西总是差不多，都是些给孩子们的食物、玩具弹珠，还有一些简单的衣物。
t_en: The contents are always roughly the same: Food, toys, and simple clothes for children.

t_ch: 奇怪的是，每次都是不同的人把包裹交到我手上，他们都不愿意透露究竟是谁如此慷慨……
t_en: Strangely, it's never the same guy who delivers the package, and none of them would tell me who the generous donor is...

t_ch: 拜这位匿名的好心人所赐，这里的小朋友们暂时什么都不缺了。
t_en: Thanks to this anonymous kind-hearted donor, the children here have more than enough things for the time being.

t_ch: 但还有一些多出来的，我看看…是几罐奶粉，几件幼儿穿的贴身衣服，还有两套故事书。
t_en: ...which is why now we have some extra stuff. Let me see... a few tins of milk powder, a few sets of clothes for toddlers, and two sets of storybooks.

t_ch: 这些东西我实在用不到，能麻烦你帮我送给其他有需要的人吗？
t_en: I'd hate to see these go to waste. Could you help me give them to someone who needs them more?

t_ch: 包在我身上了。
t_en: Leave it to me.

t_ch: 你这是把我当成送货员了。
t_en: You're treating me like a delivery man.

t_ch: 好了，快去吧。
t_en: Alright, let's go.

t_ch: 不知道哪里能搞到「哞哞」奶粉，哎，小家伙总是哭个不停……
t_en: Where can I get some Moo-Moo baby formula? *sigh*... The little guy is crying nonstop...

t_ch: 你在为奶粉发愁？
t_en: Are you worried about baby formula?

t_ch: 我想给他买奶粉，也赶过好几趟市集了，但奶粉有价无市，而我工资交完房租就不剩多少了……
t_en: I want to buy baby formula. I have been to the market several times, but the milk is expensive and always in short supply, and after the rent is paid, there's not much left...

t_ch: 小家伙为什么会哭个不停啊？
t_en: Why would the little guy keep crying?

t_ch: 孩子他妈离开得早，而我又没有照顾他的本事。要是能有奶粉，也许小家伙就能消停一点儿……
t_en: His mother left him and I don't have what it takes to look after him. If only I could get some baby formula, then perhaps...

t_ch: 这几罐奶粉送给你吧。
t_en: I happen to have some baby formula. Take these.

t_ch: 欸？这么突然…谢谢你，这怎么好意思呢，实在是太感谢了。
t_en: Wait what? That's... thank you, I mean... really, thank you so very much.

t_ch: 妈妈怎么还不下班呢…呜呜……
t_en: Why hasn't mummy finished work yet... *sniff* *sniff*

t_ch: 你怎么一个人在这待着？
t_en: Why are you here alone?

t_ch: 我、我才没有一个人…我在等妈妈下班。
t_en: I—I'm not alone... I'm waiting for mummy to finish work.

t_ch: 妈妈怎么还不下班呢？
t_en: Why isn't she off work yet?

t_ch: …陌、陌生人！妈妈说，不可以和陌生人搭话……
t_en: ...Stranger danger! Mummy said, "Never talk to strangers!"

t_ch: 没有大人在，太危险了…
t_en: It's dangerous for you to be here alone.

t_ch: 呜…可是、可是爸爸又一直睡在矿井里，妈妈说他还要再睡好几年才回来……
t_en: Ugh... bu—but daddy is sleeping in the mines... and mummy said he'll need to sleep a few more years before he comes back...

t_ch: 妈妈又要上班，她一个人养家很辛苦…我在这等她下班就好啦，附近的叔叔阿姨都认得我的。
t_en: Mummy's gotta work, she works hard... So I wait here. Everyone here knows me.

t_ch: 你爸爸不管你吗？
t_en: Does your dad take care of you?

t_ch: …爸爸睡在矿井里了。妈妈说，他还要再睡好几年才会回到我们身边……
t_en: ...daddy is sleeping in the mines... and mummy said he'll need to sleep a few more years before he comes back...

t_ch: 妈妈说，她要攒钱给我买我最喜欢的那套童话书，给我当生日礼物。不知道她是不是已经忘记了……
t_en: Mummy said, she's saving money to buy me my favorite story books for my birthday. Not sure if she forgot...

t_ch: 你妈妈托我把礼物交给你。
t_en: Your mom asked me to give you this gift.

t_ch: 哇——真的吗？太好啦，我…我也有童话书啦！
t_en: Wow! For real!? Now I finally have a fairy tale book of my own too!

t_ch: 你…你是妈妈的同事吗？之前好像从来没见过你……
t_en: Are you... are you mum's work friend? I don't think I've met you before...

t_ch: 谢谢你，{F#大姐姐}{M#大哥哥}！我就在这里待着，边看书边等妈妈下班……
t_en: Thank you! I'll just stay here and read while I wait for my mom to get off work...

t_ch: 怎么办，难道只有亲自跑一趟铆钉镇了吗……
t_en: What should I do? Must I really go to Rivet Town in person?

t_ch: 为什么要去铆钉镇？
t_en: Why go to Rivet Town?

t_ch: 欸？抱歉，你是在和我说话吗？
t_en: Eh? Excuse me, are you talking to me?

t_ch: 如果不去铆钉镇一趟的话，我妹妹就要遭罪了…唉。
t_en: If I don't make a trip to Rivet Town, my sister will suffer... ugh.

t_ch: 一个人去很危险的…
t_en: It's dangerous to go alone...

t_ch: 要是不冒这个险，妹妹她就要遭罪了…唉。
t_en: But if I don't, then my sister will be in trouble... *sigh*

t_ch: 说说看你的情况。
t_en: Tell me what happened.

t_ch: 你肯定需要幼儿衣物了。
t_en: You must need toddler clothes then.

t_ch: 欸？我还没说，你怎么知道的。
t_en: Eh? How do you know? I haven't even said anything yet.

t_ch: 我妹妹…她得了一种奇怪的皮肤病，不能穿普通的粗制亚麻衣物，否则就会全身红疹，疼痒难耐。
t_en: My sister... she has a strange skin disease and can't wear ordinary coarse linen clothes, or else a red rash will break out all over her body, which can be unbearably painful and itchy.

t_ch: 没搬出铆钉镇的时候…我们总是找当地裁缝，把布料柔软的幼儿衣物改成她能穿的贴身衣裳。
t_en: Before we moved out of Rivet Town, we always went to a local tailor to convert the soft fabrics of toddler clothes into bodysuits that she could wear.

t_ch: 镇子陷落以后，那位裁缝跟我们断了联系，布料细腻的衣服也越来越难找了……
t_en: After the town fell, we lost contact with the tailor, and it became increasingly difficult to find clothes with delicate fabrics...

t_ch: 我身为家里的大姐，必须照顾好妹妹…有必要的话，我就一个人回铆钉镇的裁缝铺里找找。
t_en: As the eldest sister in the family, I have to take good care of my younger sister... If necessary, I will go back to the tailor's shop in Rivet Town by myself to find some.

t_ch: 要不试试这几套幼儿衣服？
t_en: I do have some toddler clothes. Wanna see?

t_ch: …这么巧？能给我看看吗……
t_en: ...really? Please show me...

t_ch: 这衣物的手感…真怀念啊，我好久没触碰过这么细腻的面料了。
t_en: Oh the fabric... I really miss it, I haven't touched a fabric this delicate for such a long time.

t_ch: 那个，这几套衣服你打算怎么卖？我身上没多少钱，但如果是为了妹妹……
t_en: So how do you plan to sell these outfits? I don't have much money on me, but if it's for my sister...

t_ch: 送给你吧。
t_en: Have it for free.

t_ch: …真的吗？太感谢了，我这就回家改衣服！以后有机会的话，我请你吃我自己烤的苔藓面包，刚出炉的可香了。
t_en: ...For real? Thank you so much. I'll go home and tailor the clothes now! When the chance comes, I'd like to treat you to my home-baked moss bread. It's super yummy when fresh out of the oven.

t_ch: 咦，这是——好像有封信从衣服里掉出来了，是你的吗？请拿好……
t_en: Hey, look at this... It looks like a letter that fell out of your clothes. Is it yours?

t_ch: （给诊所寄送物资的好心人就是科鲁泽？）
t_en: (So Kluzer is the anonymous donor who sent supplies to the clinic?)

t_ch: （症状还在恶化…他很清楚自己的情况。）
t_en: (The symptoms are still worsening... he is well aware of his condition.)

t_ch: （当面问个清楚吧。）
t_en: (I should just check with him in person.)

t_ch: （科鲁泽好像不在这里，和周围的人打听下他的下落吧。）
t_en: (Kluzer doesn't seem to be here. I'll ask the people around here about his whereabouts.)

t_ch: 哎，我就不明白，「怪物盔甲」怎么会这么不经揍……
t_en: Hey, I don't understand, how could Monster Armor be beaten so easily...

t_ch: 哈哈，我早说过了，这就是五冠王拳王的含金量，懂不？
t_en: Haha, I told you so. Fighting against a five-time champ is no joke. You hear me?

t_ch: 「怪物盔甲」还在拳馆吗？
t_en: Is Monster Armor still in the boxing gym?

t_ch: 他才打完一场拳赛，输的彻彻底底！比赛一结束就灰溜溜地跑了，哈哈哈！
t_en: He just finished a match, and he was utterly destroyed! As soon as the match was over, he ran away, hahaha!

t_ch: 呃…之前还看到他在被拳馆老板教训，之后就不知道去哪儿了。
t_en: Uh... I saw him being scolded by the owner just then. Not sure where he went after.

t_ch: 干嘛，他都输成这副惨样了，不会还有人想找「怪物盔甲」要签名吧？大家都在围观五冠王，根本没空搭理那个小丑！
t_en: Monster Armor got beaten up so badly. You are not thinking about getting an autograph from him, are you? I mean, all eyes are on the five-time champ now, and no one cares about that Monster Armor clown anymore!

t_ch: 哎，「怪物盔甲」之前的战绩明明很不错的，可惜后期乏力……
t_en: But Monster Armor wasn't bad at all before. It's a shame he lost his edge...

t_ch: 科鲁泽还在拳馆吗？
t_en: Is Kluzer still here?

t_ch: 那肯定啊，又没和我解约，不在我们俱乐部还想去哪儿？
t_en: Of course he is. He's still under contract. Where else can he go?

t_ch: 这家伙是个过气又学不会研究观众口味变化的人…哼哼，我看他就是成心骗我给他发底薪！
t_en: He's just another has-been who can't adapt to the audience's tastes... Hmph. He's now just in it for the base wage that I charitably trickle down to him!

t_ch: 过气是什么意思？
t_en: What do you mean by "has-been"?

t_ch: 哈，一看你就不关注拳赛！「怪物盔甲」被五冠王收拾得体无完肤，场面惨烈得很。
t_en: Ha! You obviously don't pay much attention to our boxing matches, do you? He was beaten so badly by the five-time champ. It was such a one-sided fiasco.

t_ch: 拳手不是能打赢就行？
t_en: So boxing is all about winning?

t_ch: 哈，那可不行！能给我赚钱比能打赢比赛更重要。
t_en: Ha, winning won't be enough! Making money for me is more important than winning games.

t_ch: 再说了，他连比赛都赢不了。「怪物盔甲」被五冠王收拾得体无完肤，场面惨烈得很。
t_en: Besides, he can't even win anymore. He was beaten so badly by the five-time champ. It was such a one-sided fiasco.

t_ch: 我太懂观众了，都是群喜新厌旧的主——谁能击败人气高的选手，谁就能成为万众追捧的大英雄。
t_en: I know the audience too well. They quickly tire of the old and are constantly chasing after the new. Whoever beats the latest crowd favorite gets to be the next super star.

t_ch: 他为什么还在打拳？
t_en: Then why is he still fighting?

t_ch: 他不打拳还能去干吗？你养他？
t_en: What else can he do to put food on the table? It's not like you are gonna pay for his living.

t_ch: 他应该在家休养。
t_en: He should just take a break.

t_ch: 休息？有这个闲工夫，还不如想想如何挽救自己的人气。
t_en: A break!? He should first try to take a break from being such a loser!

t_ch: 早知道你会过来，我就不派他去裂界里找新的盔甲了。这种事还是得交给你这种专业人士来才行。
t_en: If I had known you were coming, I wouldn't have sent him to the Fragmentum to find a new set of armor. Should always leave things like this to pros like you.

t_ch: 新的盔甲？
t_en: New armor?

t_ch: 对啊，我刚才不是说了，那家伙前不久才负了伤，连盔甲都被搞坏了。
t_en: Yeah, didn't I just say, he was badly injured not long ago and even got his armor broken.

t_ch: 输家就该认识到自己跟对手的差距，这样才能体面地退场。
t_en: He should've tried to be a gracious loser and recognized the massive gap in strength between himself and his opponent.

t_ch: 但是那家伙明显还不服气，清醒过来以后就开始缠着我，让我再给他一次机会……
t_en: But that guy doesn't seem to know when to quit. Ever since he woke up from his concussion, he has been pestering me nonstop, begging me to give him another chance in the ring.

t_ch: 我今天也算是大发慈悲了——我告诉他，观众早就看腻了，你得去找个更厉害的怪物，再去搞一套跟之前不一样的盔甲来，观众才能有新鲜感。
t_en: Luckily for him, I was in a particularly merciful mood today. So I told him that the audience has been long sick of his old shtick. If he wants to win back the audience, he simply needs to find himself a much more powerful monster for a different set of armor.

t_ch: 他受伤了还能接着战斗？
t_en: Is he still fit to fight in his condition?

t_ch: 谁知道呢？我只看到那家伙拖着条伤腿，一瘸一拐地走了，多半是去找盔甲去了。
t_en: Who knows? All I saw was the guy limping away with a broken leg. He probably went looking for new armor, as he should.

t_ch: 不过管他呢，「怪物盔甲」已这个名号已经帮我吸了不少金了。他爱去哪就去哪吧！
t_en: But who cares! Monster Armor has already made me quite some money. He can go wherever he wants. After all, it's a free town here.

t_ch: 怪物的盔甲都一个样！
t_en: There're no different kinds of monster armor!

t_ch: 你真的当我没见过裂界怪物吗？我看过有些更大更可怕的怪物，要是能搞到这样的，我得花重金请他回来打拳。
t_en: You think I know nothing about Fragmentum monsters, huh? I've seen some bigger and scarier ones, and if he can get his hands on one of those, then I'm willing to pay more to rehire him.

t_ch: 我看到那家伙拖着条伤腿，一瘸一拐地走了，多半是去找盔甲去了。他最好能找个大点儿的，不然就别回来了。
t_en: I saw the guy limping away with a broken leg. He probably went looking for new armor, as he should. And he'd better find one, or else he shouldn't even bother coming back.

t_ch: 太危险了，我要去找他！
t_en: It's too dangerous. I'll go find him!

t_ch: 找怪物嘛，肯定是朝着铆钉镇方向走的。你现在出发…应该还来得及吧！
t_en: He probably headed to Rivet Town. That's where the monsters are, after all. If you leave now, you may still catch him.

t_ch: 你可真是一个大恶人。
t_en: You are so cruel.

t_ch: 哼，你要是不乐意就把他带回来嘛。他去找怪物，肯定就往铆钉镇去了。
t_en: Hmph. If you don't like this, you can just bring him back. He went looking for monsters, so he must have gone to Rivet Town.

t_ch: （告诉娜塔莎关于科鲁泽的事）
t_en: (Tell Natasha about Kluzer)

t_ch: 看来那个一直寄送包裹的好心人就是科鲁泽……
t_en: So the anonymous donor was Kluzer...

t_ch: 我记得，之前帮他做过诊断，当时他已经被那套盔甲折磨得十分虚弱了。
t_en: Oh, I remember helping him with the diagnosis when he was so weakened by that suit of armor.

t_ch: 虽然我不赞成他这样不要命的做法…但是能做到这种程度，他的意志力一定很强大。
t_en: I never approved of how he risked his life like that. I can't imagine how strong-willed he must be to manage to do all this in his condition.

t_ch: 不过，他如此拼命，又如此慷慨，究竟是为了什么呢？
t_en: But why would he risk his life this much and be this generous? All this for what, exactly?

t_ch: 为了让妻儿过上更好的生活？
t_en: To provide a better life for his wife and children?

t_ch: 但诊所收到过这么多物资，他的经济状况也并不好，这似乎有点说不通……
t_en: His financials didn't seem that good, and yet he donated so much to us at the clinic, which just doesn't make sense...

t_ch: 我也不知道…
t_en: I don't know...

t_ch: 唔…让我想想……
t_en: Hmm... let me think about this...

t_ch: 他还有留下别的什么线索吗？
t_en: Did he leave any other clues?

t_ch: （递上信件和匿名的包裹）
t_en: (you hand over Kluzer's letter and anonymous package)

t_ch: 这封信盖了邮戳又被退了回来，寄出的时间是在很久之前。
t_en: The letter was postmarked and returned a long time ago.

t_ch: 为什么已经寄出的信会被退回呢？也许…也许是因为他的妻儿已经不在那里了。
t_en: Why would this letter be returned? Maybe... maybe his wife and children are no longer there.

t_ch: 对他来说，这件事一定很难消化。好不容易找到这份活计，自己却失去了战斗的理由。
t_en: It must have been really hard for him to accept this reality. He went through all that trouble to secure a job that could finally provide for his family, only to find himself no longer having a family to provide for.

t_ch: …所以，他才会用寄送包裹的方式，把对妻儿的想念和关怀转移到诊所的孩子们身上。
t_en: ...So, what probably happened was that he redirected all his care and love to the children in this clinic, showering us with batches after batches of supplies.

t_ch: 当然了，这只是我的推论，不一定正确。
t_en: Of course, this is just my guess. This might not be the case.

t_ch: 你有见过他的妻儿吗？
t_en: Have you met his wife and children?

t_ch: 很遗憾，没有。他们也许已经……
t_en: Unfortunately, no. They might already be...

t_ch: 我们得为他做点什么…
t_en: We have to do something for him...

t_ch: 嗯。他的故事不该被遗忘。
t_en: Agreed. His story should not be forgotten.

t_ch: 我会把科鲁泽先生的事告诉孩子们。希望他们能成为跟他一样坚强又善良的大人。
t_en: I'll tell the kids about Mr. Kluzer. I hope they can grow up to be adults as strong and kind as he was.

t_ch: 当然了，故事的细节需要一些…适当的修饰。
t_en: Of course, the details of the story might just need some... proper embellishment.

t_ch: 但科鲁泽先生留下的温暖，我希望它能在地底一直传递下去……
t_en: But I do hope the kindness left by Mr. Kluzer will live long and prosper...

t_ch: 新赛季的比赛还没开始，凑热闹的都先散了吧，想报名参赛的请找史考特老板咨询详情……
t_en: The new seasonal tournament hasn't started yet, so don't loiter around. Go see Mr. Scott if you have any questions or want to sign up for the tournament.

t_ch: 史考特说我可以直接上。
t_en: Scott said I don't have to queue...

t_ch: 我有那个什么…「特殊礼遇」？
t_en: I have that... house special.

t_ch: 哟，原来是大名鼎鼎的{NICKNAME}啊。刚才忙晕了没仔细看，失敬失敬！
t_en: Hey, it's the famous {NICKNAME}! I was too busy to look carefully just now, sorry about that!

t_ch: 老板和我说过了，「特殊礼遇」其实就是贵宾级待遇嘛，您现在已经是我们拳馆的贵宾客座拳手了——只要您想比划比划，只消张张嘴，我们立刻给您安排！
t_en: Boss already told me, the house special is for VIPs, and you are definitely a VIP boxer now— anytime you want to swing a few, just say the word and we'll get someone for you!

t_ch: 您有空的话多来啊，不用客气。这不，新赛季马上要开始了，您很快就有机会在擂台上大展拳脚了！
t_en: And don't be a stranger. New season's starting soon, you can show off your new moves in no time!

t_ch: 具体规则是什么？
t_en: What are the rules?

t_ch: 您听说过「天梯排行榜」吗？也就是记录了每位拳手比赛积分的榜单。您的积分越高，在榜上排的位置也就越高。只要您在对战中取胜，就能获得一定积分。
t_en: Have you heard of the Ladder Leaderboard? It's a list recording every boxer's match points. The higher your points, the higher the rank. You will earn points for every battle you win.

t_ch: 换句话说，分数越高的拳手实力越强，您和对手皆是如此。这也意味着随着您胜利的次数增加，战斗强度也会随之增大——不过这点我猜您早有准备，对吧？
t_en: In other words, the higher the points, the stronger the boxer, for both you and your opponent. It also means that the more you win, the harder it gets. But I guess you're ready for that, right?

t_ch: 这分数具体怎么算，我们会负责搞定的，您只要专注于比赛本身就行。一旦您的排名发生变化，我们会立刻通知您。
t_en: As for the calculation of the points, we'll take care of that. All you need to do is focus on the fight itself. As soon as your ranking changes, we'll notify you.

t_ch: 真能立刻安排吗？
t_en: Can it be arranged now?

t_ch: 老板说了，您来打比赛那相当于免费给拳手们指导业务，我们巴不得您多来喂喂招儿呢。
t_en: Boss said you coming to the challenges is like free tutorials for the boxers. We wish you were here more often!

t_ch: 而且，「外来者」在下层区声名远扬——您要是肯赏脸参赛，那这拳馆指定是座无虚席！
t_en: Besides, the Outsider is well known in the Underworld. If you can grace us with your visit, then the tickets will be sold out for sure!

t_ch: 所以甭担心——赛程安排那都是小意思，我们拳馆的业务既全面又灵活，绝对没有疏漏。
t_en: So don't you worry, schedule arrangements are trivial matters. Our services are comprehensive and flexible. You will definitely be well taken care of.

t_ch: 我要参加揭幕战，可以吗？
t_en: I want to be in the opening match, is that OK?

t_ch: 没问题，当然没问题！既然是您的要求，那必须给安排上！
t_en: No problem, of course, no problem! Since it is your request, it must be arranged!

t_ch: 您稍等片刻——我这就去通知各部门，赛季揭幕战提前开始！
t_en: Just a moment, I'm going to inform all departments that the season opener is starting early!

t_ch: 本场比赛，我们隆重有请——搏击俱乐部传说中的客座拳手、来自高墙之外的超级新星——{NICKNAME}！
t_en: Coming up, we are honored by a legendary guest fighter from the Fight Club, a superstar from beyond the walls— {NICKNAME}!

t_ch: 而{M#他}{F#她}将要面对的是——搏击俱乐部中素有「新人克星」之称的自动机兵双子——「希罗」与「尤尔」！
t_en: And what {M#he}{F#she} will face is — Silo and Yur, the two automatic robot soldiers known in the Fight Club as the Rookie Busters!

t_ch: 此场战斗中，我们的超级新星能否保持上次比赛中的高水准，击败面对新手未尝败绩的「新人克星」？让我们拭目以待！
t_en: Will our superstar maintain {M#his}{F#her} level like the last match and beat the undefeatable Rookie Busters in this game? Let's wait and see!

t_ch: 各位观众，接下来千万别把你们的目光移开——比赛，现在正式开始！
t_en: Dear viewers, no time to blink— the match starts now!

t_ch: 我先去其他地方转转。
t_en: I'll go mill around elsewhere first.

t_ch: 没问题，您要是有想法了随时和我说！
t_en: No problem, come back any time!

t_ch: 这真是…这真是千古难逢的场面啊——要知道，「希罗」和「尤尔」在面对新人拳手时从未落于下风，可这次竟然被{NICKNAME}一举报废了！
t_en: This really is... this is a once-in-a-lifetime show— Silo and Yur have never been at a disadvantage against a rookie fighter, but this time they were demolished by {NICKNAME} in one go!

t_ch: 啊啊…两台自动机兵恐怕要赛季报销了——但在这个弱肉强食的舞台上，败者只有接受自己的命运，别无选择！
t_en: Ahhh... I'm afraid that the two bots are done for the season, but in this part of the jungle, losers have no choice but to accept their own destiny!

t_ch: 让我们高呼赢家的名字——{NICKNAME}——恭喜{M#他}{F#她}在本场比赛中酣畅大胜！
t_en: Let me hear you say {M#his}{F#her} name— {NICKNAME}! Congratulations on a phenomenal win in the opening!

t_ch: 您现在在天梯榜上的位置是…第<unbreak>128</unbreak>名！这可真是个不错的开始。当然，赢家的奖金是少不了的——请拿好！记住，这里的每一分钱可都出自史考特老板慷慨的腰包！
t_en: Your current ranking is... No. <unbreak>128</unbreak>! That's a great start. Of course the winner's prize is yours, here you go! And don't forget, every penny comes from the generous Mr. Scott's pockets!

t_ch: 如果您想继续比赛就请随时和我说，我来为您安排！
t_en: If you want to continue fighting, please let me know and I will arrange a match for you!

t_ch: 哈哈，欢迎我们的贵宾——{NICKNAME}！您准备好参加下一场比赛了吗？
t_en: Haha, welcome our guest of honor— {NICKNAME}! Are you ready for your next game?

t_ch: 具体规则是什么？
t_en: What are the rules?

t_ch: 您听说过「天梯排行榜」吗？也就是记录了每位拳手比赛积分的榜单。您的积分越高，在榜上排的位置也就越高。只要您在对战中取胜，就能获得一定积分。
t_en: Have you heard of the Ladder Leaderboard? It's a list recording every boxer's match points. The higher your points, the higher the rank. You will earn points for every battle you win.

t_ch: 换句话说，分数越高的拳手实力越强，您和对手皆是如此。这也意味着随着您胜利的次数增加，战斗强度也会随之增大——不过这点我猜您早有准备，对吧？
t_en: In other words, the higher the points, the stronger the boxer, for both you and your opponent. It also means that the more you win, the harder it gets. But I guess you're ready for that, right?

t_ch: 这分数具体怎么算，我们会负责搞定的，您只要专注于比赛本身就行。一旦您的排名发生变化，我们会立刻通知您。
t_en: As for the calculation of the points, we'll take care of that. All you need to do is focus on the fight itself. As soon as your ranking changes, we'll notify you.

t_ch: 真能立刻安排吗？
t_en: Can it be arranged now?

t_ch: 老板说了，您来打比赛那相当于免费给拳手们指导业务，我们巴不得您多来喂喂招儿呢。
t_en: Boss said you coming to the challenges is like free tutorials for the boxers. We wish you were here more often!

t_ch: 而且，「外来者」在下层区声名远扬——您要是肯赏脸参赛，那这拳馆指定是座无虚席！
t_en: Besides, the Outsider is well known in the Underworld. If you can grace us with your visit, then the tickets will be sold out for sure!

t_ch: 所以甭担心——赛程安排那都是小意思，我们拳馆的业务既全面又灵活，绝对没有疏漏。
t_en: So don't you worry, schedule arrangements are trivial matters. Our services are comprehensive and flexible. You will definitely be well taken care of.

t_ch: 我要参加下一场比赛。
t_en: I want to be in the next match.

t_ch: 没问题！我这就给您安排加赛，还请您稍作等候。
t_en: No problem! I'll arrange an extra match for you right now, please wait a moment.

t_ch: 各位观众，请看！坐镇擂台西角的，正是在磐岩镇引起巨大轰动、武艺高超的新秀战士——{NICKNAME}！
t_en: Next up, we have! Sitting in the west corner of the ring, the rookie fighter who caused a huge stir in Boulder Town with {M#his}{F#her} superb skills and moves— {NICKNAME}!

t_ch: 而在{M#他}{F#她}面前的又是本赛季中另一匹黑马——来自熔锤镇的年轻机械师「小智」、以及他会放电机器人搭档「哔咔啾」！他们的目标正是本赛季冠军！
t_en: And in front of {M#him}{F#her} is another dark horse of this season — the young Mechanic Ash from Moltammerville, and his discharge robot partner Beet-Ka-Choo! Their goal is to be the season champions!

t_ch: {NICKNAME}会无情地粉碎对手的梦想，还是成为年轻有为的机械师寻梦之路的垫脚石？这场战斗将为各位观众揭晓答案！
t_en: Will {NICKNAME} ruthlessly crush their opponent's dreams, or be a stepping stone to a young and promising Mechanic's dream? This battle will reveal the answer to the audience!

t_ch: 现在，我宣布——比赛开始！
t_en: Now, I declare— let the match begin!

t_ch: 我先去其他地方转转。
t_en: I'll go mill around elsewhere first.

t_ch: 没问题，您要是有想法了随时和我说！
t_en: No problem, come back any time!

t_ch: 看啊，多惨烈的场面！我们的「外来者」{NICKNAME}毫不手下留情！机械师和他的搭档倒在了逐梦之路上——他们还有再起的可能吗？
t_en: Behold, what a tragedy! Our Outsider {NICKNAME} showed no mercy! The mechanic and his partner fell on the road to their dream. Is there a chance for them to make a comeback?

t_ch: 开玩笑的，这里根本没人在意输家——让我们把目光聚焦在本场比赛的胜者身上！观众们，请把你们最热烈的掌声献给今天的胜利者——{NICKNAME}！
t_en: Just kidding, no one cares about losers here. Let's focus on the winner of this match! Viewers, give it up to our winner of the day— {NICKNAME}!

t_ch: 您现在在天梯榜上的位置是…第<unbreak>71</unbreak>名！这排名真是突飞猛进啊。当然，这次的胜利也伴随一笔丰厚奖金。记住，钱包想快乐，就找史考特！
t_en: Your current ranking is... No. <unbreak>71</unbreak>! That's a great jump. Of course the winner's prize is yours for the taking, here you go! And remember, if you know what you've got, come to Scott!

t_ch: 如果您想继续比赛就请随时和我说，我来为您安排！
t_en: If you want to continue fighting, please let me know and I will arrange a match for you!

t_ch: 哟，您来的真巧！下一场比赛正在筹备当中，您打算参加吗？
t_en: Hey, great timing! The next match is about to begin, would you like to participate?

t_ch: 具体规则是什么？
t_en: What are the rules?

t_ch: 您听说过「天梯排行榜」吗？也就是记录了每位拳手比赛积分的榜单。您的积分越高，在榜上排的位置也就越高。只要您在对战中取胜，就能获得一定积分。
t_en: Have you heard of the Ladder Leaderboard? It's a list recording every boxer's match points. The higher your points, the higher the rank. You will earn points for every battle you win.

t_ch: 换句话说，分数越高的拳手实力越强，您和对手皆是如此。这也意味着随着您胜利的次数增加，战斗强度也会随之增大——不过这点我猜您早有准备，对吧？
t_en: In other words, the higher the points, the stronger the boxer, for both you and your opponent. It also means that the more you win, the harder it gets. But I guess you're ready for that, right?

t_ch: 这分数具体怎么算，我们会负责搞定的，您只要专注于比赛本身就行。一旦您的排名发生变化，我们会立刻通知您。
t_en: As for the calculation of the points, we'll take care of that. All you need to do is focus on the fight itself. As soon as your ranking changes, we'll notify you.

t_ch: 真能立刻安排吗？
t_en: Can it be arranged now?

t_ch: 老板说了，您来打比赛那相当于免费给拳手们指导业务，我们巴不得您多来喂喂招儿呢。
t_en: Boss said you coming to the challenges is like free tutorials for the boxers. We wish you were here more often!

t_ch: 而且，「外来者」在下层区声名远扬——您要是肯赏脸参赛，那这拳馆指定是座无虚席！
t_en: Besides, the Outsider is well known in the Underworld. If you can grace us with your visit, then the tickets will be sold out for sure!

t_ch: 所以甭担心——赛程安排那都是小意思，我们拳馆的业务既全面又灵活，绝对没有疏漏。
t_en: So don't you worry, schedule arrangements are trivial matters. Our services are comprehensive and flexible. You will definitely be well taken care of.

t_ch: 我要参加下一场比赛。
t_en: I want to be in the next match.

t_ch: 没问题！我这就给您安排加赛。
t_en: No problem! A match for you right away.

t_ch: 庆贺吧，欢呼吧！因为，各位观众将在今晚见证王者归来——
t_en: Let me hear your roars! Because tonight you will witness the return of the king —

t_ch: 没错，它正是俱乐部历史上最具传奇色彩的机器人格斗家「灰背鲸」——此时此刻，带着它全新的遥控支援系统「轻骑兵」，重返这个传奇擂台！
t_en: That's right, it is the most legendary robot fighter in the club's history, Grayback Whale. And right now, equipped with a new Light Cavalry remote support system, it is returning to this legendary ring!

t_ch: 是哪位选手如此倒霉，竟然碰上如此恐怖的对手？没错，正是那位近期在俱乐部天梯榜上高歌猛进的新秀！掌声有请，不带感情的「异界拳王」——{NICKNAME}！
t_en: And who's the unlucky one to be facing such a fearful opponent? That's right. It's the rookie who has made great strides on the club Leaderboard recently! Give it up for the emotionless Limbo Champion— {NICKNAME}!

t_ch: 面对火力全开的机械战将，{NICKNAME}该何去何从？屏住呼吸吧，现场的各位观众！比赛现在正式开始！
t_en: Facing the full firepower of this mechanical warrior, what should {NICKNAME} do? Hold your breath, everyone in the audience! The game officially starts now!

t_ch: 我先去其他地方转转。
t_en: I'll go mill around elsewhere first.

t_ch: 没问题，您要是有想法了随时和我说！
t_en: No problem, come back any time!

t_ch: 怎会如此？！{NICKNAME}面对如此凶悍的对手竟毫不示弱，甚至在战斗中得心应手，将「灰背鲸」和它的「轻骑兵」们轻松击溃……
t_en: How can this be so!? {NICKNAME} showed no signs of weakness in the face of such a fierce opponent, and even handled the battle with ease, defeating the Grayback Whale and its Light Cavalries with ease...

t_ch: 这是何等的强而有力！观众们啊，将你们的喝彩献给今天的赢家吧——让我们共同纪念这场足以载入搏击俱乐部历史的精彩对决吧！
t_en: What a marvelous power to behold! Spectators, give your applause to today's winner. Let's commemorate a fight that will go down in the Fight Club's history together!

t_ch: 您现在在天梯榜上的位置是…第<unbreak>17</unbreak>名！这可是俱乐部的顶级拳手们才能达到的高度…我稍后就把这场比赛的奖金转给您——史考特一开口，就知有没有！
t_en: Your current position on the ladder is... No. <unbreak>17</unbreak>! That's the heights the club's top boxers can achieve... I'll pass on the prize money for this fight to you later. Scott will see if he has it!

t_ch: 如果您想继续比赛就请随时和我说，我来为您安排！
t_en: If you want to continue fighting, please let me know and I will arrange a match for you!

t_ch: 哈哈，欢迎我们的大明星{NICKNAME}！你现在已经是俱乐部的焦点了，大家都盼着你上擂台呢。
t_en: Haha, well if it isn't our big star, {NICKNAME}! You're the big draw for the club now, everyone is looking forward to you taking the ring.

t_ch: 具体规则是什么？
t_en: What are the rules?

t_ch: 您听说过「天梯排行榜」吗？也就是记录了每位拳手比赛积分的榜单。您的积分越高，在榜上排的位置也就越高。只要您在对战中取胜，就能获得一定积分。
t_en: Have you heard of the Ladder Leaderboard? It's a list recording every boxer's match points. The higher your points, the higher the rank. You will earn points for every battle you win.

t_ch: 换句话说，分数越高的拳手实力越强，您和对手皆是如此。这也意味着随着您胜利的次数增加，战斗强度也会随之增大——不过这点我猜您早有准备，对吧？
t_en: In other words, the higher the points, the stronger the boxer, for both you and your opponent. It also means that the more you win, the harder it gets. But I guess you're ready for that, right?

t_ch: 这分数具体怎么算，我们会负责搞定的，您只要专注于比赛本身就行。一旦您的排名发生变化，我们会立刻通知您。
t_en: As for the calculation of the points, we'll take care of that. All you need to do is focus on the fight itself. As soon as your ranking changes, we'll notify you.

t_ch: 真能立刻安排吗？
t_en: Can it be arranged now?

t_ch: 老板说了，您来打比赛那相当于免费给拳手们指导业务，我们巴不得您多来喂喂招儿呢。
t_en: Boss said you coming to the challenges is like free tutorials for the boxers. We wish you were here more often!

t_ch: 而且，「外来者」在下层区声名远扬——您要是肯赏脸参赛，那这拳馆指定是座无虚席！
t_en: Besides, the Outsider is well known in the Underworld. If you can grace us with your visit, then the tickets will be sold out for sure!

t_ch: 所以甭担心——赛程安排那都是小意思，我们拳馆的业务既全面又灵活，绝对没有疏漏。
t_en: So don't you worry, schedule arrangements are trivial matters. Our services are comprehensive and flexible. You will definitely be well taken care of.

t_ch: 我要参加下一场比赛。
t_en: I want to be in the next match.

t_ch: 没问题！我这就给您安排加赛。
t_en: No problem! A match for you right away.

t_ch: 俱乐部的新星{NICKNAME}不负众望，终于再次踏上擂台！{M#他}{F#她}一路摧枯拉朽，来到天梯榜的第十七位——此处强者云集，其中何人将与{M#他}{F#她}对垒？
t_en: The club's new star, {NICKNAME}, isn't disappointing us — {M#he}{F#she} is stepping into the ring once more! {M#He}{F#She} had no trouble with the previous opponents on {M#his}{F#her} way up to rank <unbreak>17</unbreak>. And now, which tough challenger will be facing off against {M#him}{F#her}?

t_ch: 答案或许出人意料——此刻站在擂台上的，竟是来自遥远矿镇的传奇机械武斗家「尊者」，和它那同样强悍的徒弟「多蒙」！
t_en: The answer might surprise you: Standing in the ring is a legendary mechanical warrior from the faraway mining town — Master! And its equally formidable disciple — Domon!

t_ch: 面对变化多端的「机械格斗流」正统传人，我们的超级新星是否还能招架得住？
t_en: Will our superstar be able to emerge victorious against these mighty inheritors of the unpredictable "mechanical fighting style"!?

t_ch: 请睁大眼睛吧，观众们——精彩的对战即将上演！比赛，正式开始！
t_en: Don't blink, or you might miss a moment of this brilliant spectacle! Now, let the fight begin!

t_ch: 我先去其他地方转转。
t_en: I'll go mill around elsewhere first.

t_ch: 没问题，您要是有想法了随时和我说！
t_en: No problem, come back any time!

t_ch: 哎呀呀，真是不得了！今天的这场战斗，恐怕宣告了「机械格斗流」的彻底失败，真是奇耻大辱！
t_en: Wow, that was a—ma—zing! Today's battle nailed the total failure of the "mechanical fighting style." What a shame!

t_ch: 在座的各位里又有谁能想到，如此高深莫测的古代格斗术，竟然有被「外来者」破解的一天？真是令人惊奇无比的场面！
t_en: Who would have thought that such an unfathomable ancient martial art would be cracked by an Outsider one day? What a spectacular showdown!

t_ch: 经此史诗大捷，{NICKNAME}即将来到天梯榜的第二位——这是多么傲人的战绩啊！
t_en: After this epic victory, {NICKNAME} is on the verge of reaching No. 2 on the ladder — what a feat!

t_ch: 观众们，请不要吝惜你们的掌声与喝彩！让胜利者沐浴在胜利的荣耀之中吧！
t_en: Please, everyone, shower your cheers and applause for the winner for {M#his}{F#her} victorious glory!

t_ch: 您现在在天梯榜上的位置是…哇哇哇，第2名！天梯榜首的位置近在眼前，下层区拳王的金腰带几乎唾手可得！
t_en: Your current ranking is... wow wow wow, No. 2! You're so close to the top now. The golden belt of the Underworld champion is almost within reach!

t_ch: 这场比赛的奖金很快就会转到您的账户上——史考特商业，只为冠军而生！
t_en: The prize money for this game will be transferred to your account shortly. Scott Business Alliance, made for the champions!

t_ch: 如果您想继续比赛就请随时和我说，我来为您安排！
t_en: If you want to continue fighting, please let me know and I will arrange a match for you!

t_ch: {NICKNAME}，欢迎回到俱乐部！
t_en: {NICKNAME}, welcome back to the club!

t_ch: 您现在距离登顶仅一步之遥，本赛季也已接近尾声——您的对手，也就是当前的天梯榜榜首，也已经做好准备，只等与您一战。
t_en: You are now only one step away from reaching the top, and the season is drawing to a close — your opponent, the current leader of the ladder, is also ready for you.

t_ch: 如果您准备好了就请随时和我说，我来为您安排最后一场战斗。
t_en: Feel free to let me know if you're ready and I'll arrange the final battle for you.

t_ch: 具体规则是什么？
t_en: What are the rules?

t_ch: 您听说过「天梯排行榜」吗？也就是记录了每位拳手比赛积分的榜单。您的积分越高，在榜上排的位置也就越高。只要您在对战中取胜，就能获得一定积分。
t_en: Have you heard of the Ladder Leaderboard? It's a list recording every boxer's match points. The higher your points, the higher the rank. You will earn points for every battle you win.

t_ch: 换句话说，分数越高的拳手实力越强，您和对手皆是如此。这也意味着随着您胜利的次数增加，战斗强度也会随之增大——不过这点我猜您早有准备，对吧？
t_en: In other words, the higher the points, the stronger the boxer, for both you and your opponent. It also means that the more you win, the harder it gets. But I guess you're ready for that, right?

t_ch: 这分数具体怎么算，我们会负责搞定的，您只要专注于比赛本身就行。一旦您的排名发生变化，我们会立刻通知您。
t_en: As for the calculation of the points, we'll take care of that. All you need to do is focus on the fight itself. As soon as your ranking changes, we'll notify you.

t_ch: 真能立刻安排吗？
t_en: Can it be arranged now?

t_ch: 老板说了，您来打比赛那相当于免费给拳手们指导业务，我们巴不得您多来喂喂招儿呢。
t_en: Boss said you coming to the challenges is like free tutorials for the boxers. We wish you were here more often!

t_ch: 而且，「外来者」在下层区声名远扬——您要是肯赏脸参赛，那这拳馆指定是座无虚席！
t_en: Besides, the Outsider is well known in the Underworld. If you can grace us with your visit, then the tickets will be sold out for sure!

t_ch: 所以甭担心——赛程安排那都是小意思，我们拳馆的业务既全面又灵活，绝对没有疏漏。
t_en: So don't you worry, schedule arrangements are trivial matters. Our services are comprehensive and flexible. You will definitely be well taken care of.

t_ch: 我要参加最后一场比赛。
t_en: I'm ready for the last round.

t_ch: 好的，我这就为您安排。
t_en: Alrighty. Let me get it ready for you right away.

t_ch: 传说中的外来者一路披荆斩棘，终于攀升到了到了天梯排行榜的第二位！冠军宝座近在眼前，但{M#他}{F#她}能够像往常一样轻易击败擂主吗？让我们拭目以待！
t_en: The legendary Outsider has climbed {M#his}{F#her} way to the very top, sitting at No. 2 on the Leaderboard! The championship is so close at hand, but will {M#he}{F#she} beat the champ as easily as usual? Let us wait and see!

t_ch: 而此刻坐镇天梯之巅的，竟然是与{NICKNAME}打过照面的老面孔——
t_en: And right now sitting at the top of the ladder, is actually an old friend of {NICKNAME}—

t_ch: 「灰背鲸」将其遥控支援系统从「轻骑兵」升级到了「龙骑兵」——听听，这名字多唬人啊！
t_en: Grayback Whale upgraded its remote control support system from Light Cavalry to Dragon Rider — listen to that. What a name!

t_ch: 但你们可别以为这是吓唬小孩子的花把式！短短数天的时间，「灰背鲸」以惊人的速度登上了天梯的榜首，期间未曾落得一败！
t_en: But this is no kiddie trick! In only a few days' time, Grayback Whale has surged up to No. 1 without a single defeat!

t_ch: 这场对决作为赛季的落幕战，将会决定终极大奖花落谁家——榜首宝座是否会在这最后关头再度易手，将成为本战的最大看点！
t_en: As the closing battle of the season, the showdown is guaranteed to be the season's ultimate highlight — will the championship change hands at this very last moment?

t_ch: 现在，让我们将热烈的掌声献给两位斗士——我宣布，顶峰争霸战正式开始！
t_en: Now, give it up for the two warriors — the battle for the top begins, now!

t_ch: 我先去其他地方转转。
t_en: I'll go mill around elsewhere first.

t_ch: 没问题，您要是有想法了随时和我说！
t_en: No problem, come back any time!

t_ch: 我去，我去，我去！赛场上的场面已经彻底失控了！「灰背鲸」竟然在关键时刻掉了链子，在这最后关头将冠军的宝座拱手相让——
t_en: Oh gosh oh gosh oh gosh! The battlefield is completely out of control! The Grayback Whale lost it at the very last moment and kissed the championship a regretful goodbye—

t_ch: {NICKNAME}！{NICKNAME}！{NICKNAME}！这是何等压倒性的实力，何等华丽的胜利——在「异界拳魔」的面前，战栗吧，颤抖吧！
t_en: {NICKNAME}! {NICKNAME}! {NICKNAME}! What an overwhelming power, what a magnificent victory — All kneel to the Limbo Monster. Tremble. Tremble!

t_ch: 经此一役，磐岩镇搏击俱乐部迎来了进化之日！无限制格斗的新时代即将到来——而促成这一伟业的，正是本赛季的终极赢家，{NICKNAME}！
t_en: This battle welcomes a new era of evolution for the Boulder Town Fight Club! A new era for freestyle fighting has dawned upon us, and it's pushed by this season's ultimate winner — {NICKNAME}!

t_ch: 欢呼吧，庆贺吧！你们有幸见证了新王的诞生，搏击俱乐部的未来在此刻到来！
t_en: Cheer and celebrate! You're all lucky witnesses to the birth of a new king, and a future of the Fight Club!

t_ch: 这个赛季已经圆满结束了，恭喜您成为冠军！您自然应得到丰厚的奖励——史考特老板早就安排好了，请您一定拿好！
t_en: The season has come to a close, congratulations on your championship! And of course Mr. Scott has arranged for your rich reward. Here you go!

t_ch: 记住——史考特商业联盟，只为最强的你！
t_en: Remember, Scott Business Alliance is only made for you, the strongest!

t_ch: 接下来的一段时间，搏击俱乐部会暂时歇业，等待新赛季的来临！相关的前期工作正在紧密筹备中，敬请期待！
t_en: Now the Fight Club will be temporarily closed. We'll be working hard behind the scenes to prepare for the next season! Stay tuned!

t_ch: 看，诊所附近聚集了好多人呀！
t_en: Look! There's a big crowd around the clinic.

t_ch: 难道他们也被刚才的战斗波及到了…快过去问问看吧。
t_en: Do you think they felt the effects of the battle just now...? Let's go ask around.

t_ch: 娜塔莎，奥列格！久等了啦！
t_en: Natasha! Oleg! Hope you didn't have to wait too long.

t_ch: 啊——他们回来了！
t_en: Oh! It's them!

t_ch: 没想到这么快！看精神状态，各位带来的应该是好消息吧？
t_en: You kids move fast! By the look on your faces, I assume you come bearing good news?

t_ch: 那是当然！你肯定想象不到，我们费了好～大的力气——
t_en: Mm-hmm, you bet! You might not believe this, but we summoned up all our strength, and then...

t_ch: …呃，还是{NICKNAME}来说吧，我不擅长汇报工作，说错话就尴尬了。
t_en: ...Uh, maybe {NICKNAME} should do the talking. I'm no good at serious stuff — I'll probably say something I'll regret.

t_ch: 守护者牺牲自己封印了星核。
t_en: The guardian sacrificed herself to seal the Stellaron.

t_ch: 我们参与了战斗，见证了整个过程。
t_en: We were there at the fight — we witnessed everything.

t_ch: 你们两个还真是面不改色呀…难道这是拥有神秘过去的人必备的素养吗？
t_en: "Sacrificed herself," huh... Sure. Yeah. That's the whole story...

t_ch: 丹恒，要不还是你说吧…
t_en: Do you feel like recounting the story, Dan Heng...?

t_ch: 大守护者可可利亚牺牲了自己，封印了「星核」。我们参与了战斗，见证了整个过程。
t_en: The supreme guardian sacrificed herself to seal the Stellaron. We were there at the fight — we witnessed everything.

t_ch: 丹恒还真是面不改色呀…不愧是隐瞒了自己神秘过去的家伙。
t_en: "Sacrificed herself," huh... Sure. Yeah. That's the whole story...

t_ch: 可可利亚？她为了贝洛伯格…唔…我明白了。
t_en: Cocolia? In order to save Belobog, she... I see.

t_ch: 所以，下层区此前感受到的剧烈震动，就是那场战斗导致的，对吗？
t_en: So the massive tremors we felt earlier were a result of the battle, right?

t_ch: 是的，胜利来之不易。
t_en: Yes, it was a hard-won victory.

t_ch: 其实我们才是最大功臣…
t_en: We quite literally saved the world...

t_ch: …你真会挑时候邀功！
t_en: ...Through patting yourself on the back!?

t_ch: …谢谢你们。虽然很难想象你们究竟在上面经历了什么，但万幸结果是好的。
t_en: ...Thank you. I can't even imagine what you've been through up there. I'm just glad everything turned out alright.

t_ch: 那希儿她？
t_en: What about Seele?

t_ch: 放心吧，她没事！布洛妮娅身体抱恙，希儿先带她去找铁卫了。
t_en: Don't worry, she's alright! It took a big toll on Bronya, so Seele took her over to the Guards.

t_ch: 那就好…你们都没事真是太好了。
t_en: Good... I'm so glad you're all okay.

t_ch: 可可利亚牺牲了，那个铁卫姑娘就会接过「大守护者」的位子吧？
t_en: Now that Cocolia is no more, I guess our Silvermane girl is about to become the next supreme guardian?

t_ch: 她是个意志坚定，言出必行的姑娘。有了她的帮助，下层区一定能重获新生。
t_en: She sees things through and doesn't go back on her word. The Underworld will thrive again under her watch.

t_ch: 嗯！我们也带了布洛妮娅的口信来哦——「封锁很快就会结束，下层区的人们很快就能自由地呼吸了」。
t_en: Mm-hmm! Oh, we brought you a message from Bronya! Ahem, "The blockade will soon be lifted. The people of the Underworld will breathe freely."

t_ch: 「自由地呼吸」…没错，那就是我们一直以来的愿望。
t_en: "Breathe freely..." That's been our wish all along.

t_ch: 三月，丹恒还有{NICKNAME}——能再请你们帮一个忙吗？我想把象征希望的好消息告诉大家，缓解紧张情绪。
t_en: March, Dan Heng, and {NICKNAME}... can I ask for your help again? I want to get this news out to everyone. It'll lift their spirits and bring them hope.

t_ch: 非常乐意。
t_en: Count us in.

t_ch: 很期待人们的表情。
t_en: I can't wait to see their faces.

t_ch: 谢谢你们。该先去找谁就请各位决定吧，其余的交给地火就好。
t_en: Thank you. Choose whoever you like. Wildfire will take care of the rest.

t_ch: 下层区跟我们打过交道的人可真不少呢…{NICKNAME}，你觉得该先去找谁呢？
t_en: We've gotten to know a lot of Underworlders... {NICKNAME}, who do you think we should start with?

t_ch: 先通知大矿区的矿队吧。
t_en: Let's start with the mining team over at the Great Mine.

t_ch: 去找虎克和鼹鼠党吧。
t_en: Let's start with Hook and The Moles.

t_ch: 先去通知歌蒂吧。
t_en: Let's start with Gertie.

t_ch: 我明白了，剩下的人就交由我们通知吧。如果一切顺利…这应该就是「地火」最后的任务了。
t_en: Roger that — you can leave the rest to us. If everything goes smoothly... this should be Wildfire's final mission.

t_ch: 辛苦你们跑这一趟了，大家应该都没事吧？
t_en: Oh, is everyone alright? Thanks for your help.

t_ch: 下次可以手机群发…
t_en: Maybe next time we can send a group notification...

t_ch: 哈哈。我觉得，当面传达的消息能承载更重要的意义吧。
t_en: Haha. Sometimes face-to-face communication is more meaningful, don't you think?

t_ch: 为你，千千万万遍。
t_en: For you, anything.

t_ch: 那我真是受宠若惊，{NICKNAME}。
t_en: I'm flattered, {NICKNAME}.

t_ch: 接下来，就是等待封锁解除的消息了。但愿布洛妮娅不会让大家等太久。
t_en: Now we just have to wait for the official news. I hope Bronya doesn't keep us too long.

t_ch: 说起来…守护者发生的变故，不知道史瓦罗和克拉拉有没有受到影响呢？
t_en: Huh... do you think Svarog and Clara felt the effects of our battle with the guardian?

t_ch: 我猜他们没事——史瓦罗对「地火」不客气，对克拉拉可是关爱有加。不过，如果你们真的担心…那不妨去机械聚落找找他们吧。
t_en: I think they'll be fine... Plus Svarog's devotion to Clara is even greater than his hostility to Wildfire. If you're worried, you could look for them over at the robot settlement.

t_ch: 大家好像都一脸慌乱的样子，希望旅店那边没出什么事……
t_en: Why is everyone in such a hurry? I hope nothing's happened at the hotel...

t_ch: 去看看情况吧，兴许还能帮上些忙。
t_en: We should take a look, maybe we can help.

t_ch: 我看到歌蒂了！怎么还有个人躺在地上？
t_en: I see Gertie! But who's that lying on the ground?

t_ch: 走，快上去看看吧！
t_en: Quick, let's head over!

t_ch: 哎哟，这下坏了…小伙子？小伙子——你没事吧？
t_en: Oh my, this doesn't look good... Can you hear me? Are you okay?

t_ch: 应、应该还有气儿！歌、歌蒂大婶，别、别慌……
t_en: H—He's still breathing! T—Try not to p—panic Aunt Gertie...

t_ch: 这里发生什么事了？咦，这不是匹克嘛——莫非他又在路中间睡着了？
t_en: Is everything okay? Wait, isn't that... Peak? Don't tell me he fell asleep in the middle of the road again.

t_ch: 哎，居然是你们仨！你们怎么这么快就回来下层啦？
t_en: Oh, it's you three! Back in the Underworld so soon?

t_ch: 我这一阵正好在忙着拾掇大饭店的环境布置，匹克小伙是个热心肠，听说这事之后就马上跑来帮忙了。
t_en: Word must have gotten out that I was renovating the hotel today. Being the kind-hearted young man that he is, Peak came straight over and offered to help.

t_ch: 忙着忙着，整个旅店突然开始剧烈摇晃——我赶紧带着大家跑了出来，只有匹克小伙不小心被天花板掉下来的碎瓦片砸中了……
t_en: There we were hard at work, when all of a sudden the hotel started shaking... I got everyone outside as fast as I could, but I think Peak was hit by a ceiling tile...

t_ch: 确定他当时没在偷懒吗…
t_en: Are you sure he was "hard at work...?"

t_ch: 喂，他好像醒过来啦，快看！
t_en: Look! Seems like he's coming around.

t_ch: 快，人工呼吸——
t_en: Quick, mouth-to-mouth resuscitation...

t_ch: 应该没严重到那个程度。看，他已经醒了。
t_en: I... don't think that's necessary. Look, he's awake.

t_ch: 哎…哎哟喂……
t_en: Huh...? Argh...

t_ch: 匹克小伙，你醒了？没事就好，没事就好！慢点慢点，别勉强自己……
t_en: Peak, are you awake? Oh, thank goodness you're alright! Easy now, take your time...

t_ch: 哎哟，我这老腰…刚才睡得正香呢，怎么突然就……
t_en: Argh, my back... I was having such a great nap, how did I...

t_ch: 你、你没受伤吧，哥们？我、我还是带你去娜、娜塔莎医生那儿看看吧……
t_en: A—Are you okay, bro? L—Let me take you to N—Natasha's clinic...

t_ch: 咦，你们看——匹克躺着的地方，好像有什么东西？
t_en: Hey, look where he was lying. What is that?

t_ch: 这是……
t_en: It's...

t_ch: 这是…好久之前的一张照片啊。我还以为老早就弄丢了，没想到居然……
t_en: ...an old photo. I thought I lost this ages ago. How did it get...

t_ch: 这上面的人是…歌蒂大婶，还有老歌德？看上去好年轻呀！
t_en: Aunt Gertie, isn't that you in the photo? And Old Goethe, too! You look so young!

t_ch: 可不是吗。当时我那位远房大哥才刚接过宾馆的家业没多久，他亲笔写了封信邀请我去上层参观。没想到啊，这一别就是将近二十年……
t_en: Don't I know it. My distant cousin had only just taken over the family business at the time. He wrote me a letter inviting me to head up and take a look. It's been nearly <unbreak>20</unbreak> years already...

t_ch: 唉，也不知道这辈子还有没有机会再见他一面，再到宾馆大堂去喝杯茶……
t_en: *sigh* I remember chatting in the hotel lobby and drinking tea. I wonder if we'll ever get another chance to do that...

t_ch: 关于这个…我们有个好消息想传达给你。对吧，{NICKNAME}？
t_en: ...Y'know, we've got some good news for you, haven't we, {NICKNAME}?

t_ch: 将胜利的消息分享给了歌蒂……
t_en: You tell Gertie about the victory...

t_ch: …你、你们说的都是真的？布洛妮娅姑娘她…这、这简直就像在做梦一样！
t_en: ...Is that true? Bronya, she... I—I can't believe my ears!

t_ch: 相信布洛妮娅吧。
t_en: Put your faith in Bronya.

t_ch: 你和老歌德很快就能再见面了。
t_en: You and Old Goethe will have the kettle on the stove in no time.

t_ch: 我…哈哈哈，我就说嘛！失而复得，肯定预示着有好事发生！
t_en: I... Hahaha! I don't know what to say! Turns out things aren't always lost forever. *sigh* It feels like better days are finally on the horizon!

t_ch: 谢谢你们特意来告诉我这个好消息。我现在感觉浑身充满了干劲儿…等上下层的通路重新开放，肯定会有不少人来我这儿住宿吧？
t_en: Thank you all so much for coming to tell me the good news. I haven't felt like this in a long time... We'll get plenty of guests staying at the hotel again once the passageway is open!
